headline: In search of a restaurant with extra rizz
summary: Which spaces get the party started? Our food writer reports
body_text:
The last time I went to The Dover in Mayfair, I wasn’t celebrating a birthday, but four other people were, judging by the times the waiters filed out to sing “Happy Birthday” carrying an Italian dessert with a candle in it. What makes The Dover so good for celebrations? The dapper service, ambience and cocktail list play a role. But so does the seductive lighting, which is guaranteed to make diners turning any age look a million bucks. For glamour, it’s on a par with The Benjamin in Los Angeles, the Polo Bar in New York and Bob Bob Ricard in London, where pressing one’s finger to the champagne button has become the photo op equivalent of blowing out the candles on one’s cake.

Food should be a factor. But perhaps because paying homage to a chef’s creations can feel antithetical to the party spirit, food is often secondary to the vibe. “I’d rather go to The Fat Duck one random Thursday than book it for a birthday,” one gourmand friend told me. “There is just too much pressure to have a
